fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Tennessee Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Tennessee - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity tennessee, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to tennessee, pharmacy technician compact tennessee, tennessee pharmacy technician endorsement application

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Tennessee.

steps.title: 1. Review Eligibility and Requirements
steps.description: Before applying, ensure you meet all eligibility requirements, including being 18 years of age or older (or having a high school diploma/GED) and not having a felony conviction. Review the full requirements on the Tennessee Board of Pharmacy's technician page at tn.gov/health/pharmacy-board/technician.

steps.title: 2. Create an Account on the HLRS Portal
steps.description: Go to the Tennessee Health Licensure and Regulatory System (HLRS) at apps.tn.gov/hlrs/. Click on 'New User Registration' and follow the instructions to create your profile. You will need to provide your Social Security Number, date of birth, and an active email address.

steps.title: 3. Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your newly created HLRS account. Select the option to 'Apply for a New License' and choose 'Pharmacy Technician' from the list of professions. Complete all sections of the application form accurately.

steps.title: 4. Complete Criminal Background Check (CBC)
steps.description: During the online application process, you will be provided with instructions to complete a criminal background check (CBC) through the state-approved vendor, Identogo. You must follow these instructions to schedule and complete the fingerprinting process.

steps.title: 5. Upload Required Documents
steps.description: Electronically upload all necessary supporting documents as prompted within the HLRS system. This typically includes a Declaration of US Citizenship and Lawful Presence form, and potentially proof of high school graduation or GED if applicable. Ensure all documents are clear and legible.

steps.title: 6. Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $50 using a credit or debit card through the secure online payment system within the HLRS portal to finalize your application submission.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the PTCB. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Tennessee pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Tennessee have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Tennessee. To work in Tennessee, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Tennessee?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Tennessee while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in Tennessee
hero.dek: Apply for a Pharmacy Technician license online through the Tennessee Health Licensure and Regulatory System (HLRS) at apps.tn.gov/hlrs/. Create a new user account, select Pharmacy Technician from the application options, and complete the required steps.

official_links.portal_name: Tennessee Health Licensure and Regulatory System (HLRS)
official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the HLRS portal and click 'New User Registration'. Follow the prompts to create an account using your personal information (SSN, DOB, email). Once registered, log in and select 'Apply for a New License', then choose 'Pharmacy Technician'.
